Stop Applying Everywhere. Apply Right. 🎯

Everyone thinks more applications = more chances.
That's exactly why most students get rejected.

Germany doesn't reward volume. It rewards strategy.

The students who got in weren't smarter.
They were smarter about WHERE they applied.

If your SOP is generic...
If your universities are random...
If your profile doesn't match...

Quality beats quantity in Germany admissions.

When shortlisting universities for your studies in Germany, consider the following factors:

German Grade Requirement 🇩🇪: Verify if you meet the specific German grade requirements for admission, as different universities may have varying grade expectations.

Language Requirements 🗣️: 
 Verify the specific language requirements for your course. Some programs may require an IELTS level of C1 with an overall band score of 7. Additionally, if you're considering studying in Germany, don't forget to check for any German language proficiency requirements.

Application Deadline 📆: Don't miss application deadlines; universities in Germany have strict cutoff dates. Ensure you submit your application on time to be considered for admission.

Restricted Courses 🚫: Some programs may have restrictions such as limited seats, competitive admission criteria, or prerequisites. Be aware of these limitations and ensure you qualify.

Unrestricted Courses ✅: Explore universities offering unrestricted courses that align with your interests and qualifications. These programs typically have more flexible admission criteria.

caption_text:
studyabroad_germany
 
Edited
•
323w
Here I am talking about the main differences in studying and learning methods I could observe while studying in Germany 🇩🇪 vs Norway 🇳🇴 1. Study blocks 
In Norway there are more (four blocks) study blocks throughout the year after each taking place exams. In Germany, in contrast, there are only two terms: Spring term and Fall term, after each one taking place exams. Probably to this aspect each would have diverse opinions: I found the fractioned study time per blocks more useful and practical. In other words, by splitting the study material 📖 into more study blocks per year is more productive, as you keep yourself busy with the study content and absorb the information so more efficiently. 
2. Course work
This one aspect brings more differences between how things are arranged in both countries. While in Germany, much focus is placed on independent, autonomous work by the student; in Norway team work assignments are encouraged and practiced. As an international, I found team assignments and projects very important and helpful, because I knew better who are my peers and felt more integrated. 
3. Classes 
The way classes are organized and held in both countries also has differences. In Norway 🇳🇴 much emphasis is put on presentation, holding class discussions, allocating time for open questions. I had always a feeling the class itself is half teacher-based lecturing and the other half is left for discussions, that bring more student engagement. ▶️continuation follows

If you have a disability or chronic illness and are considering studying or researching abroad, the DAAD can help you navigate the process. We understand that mobility can raise additional questions and sometimes incur extra costs. That's why we provide guidance and, upon request, can cover disability-related expenses that aren't funded elsewhere. Simply apply through our regular scholarship programmes, just like every other candidate.

And if going abroad in person isn't possible right now, our programme Chancen.Digital opens the door to earning a digital Master's degree with international academic partners.

Find more information under the link in our bio.

#DAAD #Inclusion #AccessibleEducation #AcademicMobility #Research #StudyAbroad #HigherEducation #ChangeByExchange

42 likes, 0 comments - daad_worldwide on December 19, 2025: "Applications for the relAI PhD Programme are now open!

AI is reshaping our world, but to unlock its full potential, we need reliable AI that is safe, secure, private and responsible. The relAI PhD programme trains future researchers in the mathematical and algorithmic foundations of reliable AI, with application areas in healthcare, robotics, algorithmic decision-making and learning & instruction.

relAI offers cross-sectional training, including scientific knowledge, professional development courses, and industrial exposure - thus providing a coherent, yet flexible and personalised training. 

Based in Munich's vibrant AI ecosystem, relAI connects PhD candidates with @tu.muenchen, @lmu.muenchen, leading international AI centres and industry partners. Funded students receive a full salary, social benefits, travel grants and mentorship by a relAI Fellow.

📅 Apply by 13 January 2026

More info on the programme under the link in our bio.

The Zuse Schools are financed with funds from the @bmbf.bund. 

#ZuseSchoolsAI #DAAD #PhDProgramme #AIResearch #AITalents #AIExcellence #ReliableAI #relAI

📸 © DAAD / Nathan Dreessen".

71 likes, 1 comments - daad_worldwide on October 16, 2025: "Thinking about taking your master's in AI to the next level?

The Konrad Zuse School of Excellence in Learning and Intelligent Systems (ELIZA) offers scholarships for outstanding master's students from Germany and around the world - and particularly supports students from underrepresented groups.

Scholarships are open to students who are enrolled in, or in the process of enrolling in, a master's programme at one of the ELIZA institutions and who intend to progress to a PhD.

Your benefits as a scholarship holder:

🔷 Join an ELIZA fellow's research group
🔷 Take part in the ELIZA curriculum
🔷 Option to attend courses at other ELIZA sites

You can apply at any time - the cut-off dates each year are 25 April and 25 October.

ELIZA is a DAAD-funded graduate school coordinated by @tudarmstadt. It connects leading universities and research institutions across Germany - such as Heidelberg University, Technische Universität Berlin, and the Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems - and focuses on machine learning foundations, machine learning systems, autonomous systems, and trans-disciplinary applications.

More details via the link in our bio!

The Zuse Schools are financed with funds from the @bmftr_bund.

#DAAD #ChangeByExchange #ZuseSchoolsAI #AI #AIResearch #AITalents #DAAD

📸 © DAAD / Nathan Dreessen".

108 likes, 2 comments - daad_worldwide on May 22, 2025: "Want to advance your career in AI? Fancy a PhD?
Then apply by 30 May for one of eight paid AI Research Associate / PhD positions at SECAI Zuse School!
Financed with funds from @bmftr_bund, the Konrad Zuse School of Excellence in Embedded Composite Artificial Intelligence (SECAI) offers a first-class learning and research environment. As a Research Associate / PhD student, you will be answering challenging research questions under the supervision of leading researchers and together with other team members of the hosting universities @tudresden and @unileipzig. Plus: you can benefit from the school's network in industry and research and develop your own research profile. 

SECAI's main research focus areas:
•	Composite AI
•	AI Compute Paradigms
•	Intelligent Medical Devices
•	AI Methods for Health
•	Societal Framework of AI

Your profile:
•	Very good research-oriented university degree in a discipline that is relevant to SECAI's research fields
•	Fluency in English

The location of work (Dresden or Leipzig) depends on the topic assigned for each position. 

Find all details via the link in our bio!
#DAAD #ChangeByExchange #ZuseSchoolsAI #AI #AIResearch #AITalents #PhDPosition".
